herbs.effect: Both lower blood sugar — combined use may cause hypoglycemia
herbs.recommendation: Monitor blood glucose closely. Reduce fenugreek dose. Consult doctor.

herbs.effect: Additive blood sugar lowering
herbs.recommendation: Monitor blood sugar. May need Metformin dose adjustment.

herbs.effect: Enhanced insulin sensitivity
herbs.recommendation: Culinary amounts (1/2 tsp/day) are safe. Avoid supplements.

herbs.effect: No significant interaction. Amla may improve insulin sensitivity beneficially.
herbs.recommendation: Safe to use

herbs.effect: No significant interaction known
herbs.recommendation: Safe to use at normal doses

herbs.effect: May enhance blood pressure lowering — additive hypotensive effect
herbs.recommendation: Use lower dose (300mg). Monitor BP regularly.

herbs.effect: Garlic has antihypertensive properties — may enhance effect
herbs.recommendation: Culinary amounts fine. Avoid high-dose garlic supplements.

herbs.effect: Licorice raises blood pressure — directly counteracts ACE inhibitor
herbs.recommendation: AVOID licorice root (Yashtimadhu) while on Lisinopril

herbs.effect: May interfere with drug absorption
herbs.recommendation: Take Guggulu 2+ hours away from medication

herbs.effect: No known interaction
herbs.recommendation: Safe to use

herbs.effect: Turmeric/curcumin has antiplatelet properties — increases bleeding risk
herbs.recommendation: Culinary amounts OK. AVOID curcumin supplements while on Warfarin.

herbs.effect: Ginger inhibits platelet aggregation — may enhance anticoagulation
herbs.recommendation: Use only in cooking. Avoid ginger supplements.

herbs.effect: Mild antiplatelet activity reported
herbs.recommendation: Consult doctor before using on Warfarin

herbs.effect: Haritaki component may interact with coagulation
herbs.recommendation: Avoid Triphala while on Warfarin

herbs.effect: Amla has antiplatelet activity
herbs.recommendation: Avoid high-dose Amla supplements on Warfarin. Dietary amounts OK.

herbs.effect: Ashwagandha may increase thyroid hormone levels — can cause over-medication
herbs.recommendation: Consult endocrinologist. May need dose adjustment. Monitor thyroid levels.

herbs.effect: Guggulsterone affects thyroid function directly
herbs.recommendation: AVOID Guggulu while on thyroid medication without doctor supervision

herbs.effect: No significant thyroid interaction known
herbs.recommendation: Safe to use

herbs.effect: Take at least 4 hours apart — may affect absorption
herbs.recommendation: Separate by 4 hours from Levothyroxine dose

herbs.effect: Both affect serotonin/GABA pathways — potential additive sedation
herbs.recommendation: Use caution. Start with very low dose. Monitor for excessive drowsiness.

herbs.effect: Brahmi affects serotonin — may interact with SSRIs
herbs.recommendation: Consult psychiatrist before combining. Monitor closely.

herbs.effect: Additive CNS depression risk
herbs.recommendation: Avoid Valerian while on SSRIs
herbs.alternative: Ashwagandha in very low dose for sleep

herbs.effect: No significant SSRI interaction
herbs.recommendation: Safe to use

herbs.effect: Both have estrogenic activity — monitor for hormonal effects
herbs.recommendation: Generally safe at normal doses. Inform gynecologist.

herbs.effect: Uterine tonic — may interact with OCP hormones
herbs.recommendation: Use with caution. Inform doctor.

herbs.effect: No significant OCP interaction
herbs.recommendation: Safe to use

generalWarnings: Always inform your doctor about all herbal supplements you are taking
Herbal remedies can affect drug metabolism via CYP450 liver enzymes
Interactions can be additive (too much effect) or antagonistic (reduced drug effect)
Pregnant/breastfeeding women should not take any herbs without medical supervision
Children's dosages are different — always consult pediatric specialist
